close_reason: Algorithm is documented in from_uri doc; README auto-generated
description:
secretx-aws-kms/README.md states the URI accepts ?algorithm=ecdsa-p256 or rsa-pss-2048, but does not say:
  - The algorithm is fixed for the life of the AwsKmsBackend (cannot be changed after from_uri).
  - The algorithm is NOT validated against the actual KMS key at construction; mismatches surface only at first sign() call (see F3).
  - Ed25519 is unsupported by AWS KMS, callers wanting Ed25519 should use a different backend.
  - 'aws-kms is signing-only; no SecretStore::get/put even though SecretStore is implemented' (see F4).

Audience: a contributor who picks up this crate from crates.io, sees secret*x*-aws-kms in the dependency list, and wonders what differs from secretx-aws-sm. Adding a 'Limitations' section with these four bullets would prevent four classes of issue reports.

What would make it clear: 4-line 'Limitations' section under Usage, plus a sentence in the Usage code block clarifying that the algorithm parameter is just a routing hint, not validation.
title: README does not document that algorithm is fixed at construction
